description: Executable spec for the autonomous-pipeline issue-label state machine (issue #236). Each entry is one LEGAL (from, event, guards[], actions[], to) tuple. This file is the SOURCE OF TRUTH for the mermaid diagram in state-machine.md (generated by scripts/gen-state-machine.sh) and is checked against the dispatcher/wrapper code by scripts/check-spec-drift.sh. Editing this file WITHOUT regenerating the diagram (or vice-versa) fails the spec-drift CI job. This file gates; it does NOT execute at dispatch time (the runtime reconciler is the gated/stop-ruled phase). See docs/pipeline/state-machine.md for the prose contract and docs/pipeline/observation-snapshot.md for the typed inputs the guards read.

transitions.mermaid: [*] --> autonomous: maintainer applies autonomous label
transitions.note: Out-of-band human action; not written by any pipeline actor.

transitions.code_site: dispatcher-tick.sh: label_swap "$issue_num" "" "in-progress"
transitions.mermaid: autonomous --> in_progress: Dispatcher Step 2 scan-new (deps resolved)
transitions.note: Deps resolved per INV-11 (state includes MERGED).

transitions.code_site: autonomous-dev.sh cleanup(): --remove-label in-progress --remove-label pending-dev --add-label pending-review
transitions.mermaid: in_progress --> pending_review: Dev wrapper trap (exit 0, PR exists)
transitions.note: Removes both in-progress and pending-dev (idempotent against residue).

transitions.code_site: autonomous-dev.sh cleanup() (no-PR + failure + startup-failure paths): --remove-label in-progress --add-label pending-dev
transitions.mermaid: in_progress --> pending_dev: Dev wrapper trap (exit 0 no PR, or exit non-zero)
transitions.note: Covers exit 0 with no PR, non-zero exit, and the startup-failure early-return path.

transitions.code_site: dispatcher-tick.sh Step 5a: label_swap "$issue_num" "in-progress" "pending-review"
transitions.mermaid: in_progress --> pending_review: Dispatcher Step 5a (ALIVE+PR ready, idle 5min, CI green) and wrapper trap converge (INV-15 PR-6)
transitions.note: Convergent with the wrapper SIGTERM trap (RECEIVED_SIGTERM rewrite, PR-6).

transitions.code_site: autonomous-dev.sh cleanup() SIGTERM-with-no-PR path → pending-dev
transitions.mermaid: in_progress --> pending_dev: Step 5a SIGTERM with no PR (operator kill / orphan)
transitions.note: SIGTERM with no PR (operator kill / orphan) routes to pending-dev.

transitions.code_site: dispatcher-tick.sh Step 5b: label_swap "$issue_num" "in-progress" "pending-review"
transitions.mermaid: in_progress --> pending_review: Dispatcher Step 5b (DEAD+PR, new commits)
transitions.note: current headRefOid != last Reviewed HEAD trailer.

transitions.code_site: dispatcher-tick.sh Step 5b: label_swap "$issue_num" "in-progress" "pending-dev"
transitions.mermaid: in_progress --> pending_dev: Dispatcher Step 5b (DEAD+PR, no new commits)
transitions.note: current headRefOid == last Reviewed HEAD trailer.

transitions.code_site: dispatcher-tick.sh Step 5b: label_swap "$issue_num" "in-progress" "pending-dev"
transitions.mermaid: in_progress --> pending_dev: Dispatcher Step 5b (DEAD, no PR)
transitions.note: DEAD requires pid_alive miss AND no near-success PR signal AND no recent heartbeat (INV-24).

transitions.code_site: dispatcher-tick.sh Step 3: label_swap "$issue_num" "pending-review" "reviewing"
transitions.mermaid: pending_review --> reviewing: Dispatcher Step 3 scan-pending-review
transitions.note: Atomic swap (single gh issue edit) to avoid the forbidden pending-review+reviewing combo.

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh PASS auto-merge: --remove-label reviewing --remove-label autonomous --add-label approved
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> approved: Review wrapper verdict PASS (merged or manual)
transitions.note: Only site that removes autonomous. GitHub auto-closes the issue via Closes #N; the wrapper does NOT call gh issue close (INV-33).

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh INV-127 review-round-cap breaker (substantive FAIL sub-path): --remove-label reviewing --add-label stalled
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> stalled: severity ratchet's P0/P1 floor still failing after REVIEW_CONVERGENCE_CAP consecutive failed-substantive rounds [INV-127]
transitions.note: INV-127 (#449): a NEW, independent breaker — halts re-dispatch once the severity-aware blocking ratchet's own floor (P0/P1, which always blocks at any round) is STILL failing after REVIEW_CONVERGENCE_CAP (default 5) consecutive failed-substantive rounds. Runs INSIDE the existing substantive-FAIL sub-path (AGGREGATE=="fail"), BEFORE the review-fail-substantive transition below — a trip short-circuits that transition entirely via label_swap reviewing→stalled FIRST (atomic, mirrors INV-105/INV-122's TOCTOU fix), then posts exactly ONE reason=review-round-cap report. Checks the issue is not already stalled (e.g. INV-105 or INV-122 tripped first) before running. Distinct fingerprint from both siblings: INV-105 measures dev-side zero-commit-inaction on a FROZEN head; INV-122 measures a fixed-point repetition on an UNCHANGED (head, rc) pair; INV-127 measures review-side divergent-findings non-convergence across PROGRESSIVELY NEW heads (the dev agent DID push commits, but the review keeps finding a new P0/P1 each round) — so its round counter is deliberately head-AGNOSTIC (accumulates across head changes), unlike INV-122's head-scoped fingerprint. Only failed-substantive rounds count; failed-non-substantive is out of scope (already governed by REVIEW_RETRY_LIMIT).

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh FAIL routes: --remove-label reviewing --add-label pending-dev
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> pending_dev: Review wrapper verdict FAIL
transitions.note: failed-substantive: agent posted findings. Wrapper submits gh pr review --request-changes (best-effort, INV-52). INV-127's round-cap breaker above intercepts this transition when the round cap is reached.

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh PASS-but-merge-failed FAIL route: --remove-label reviewing --add-label pending-dev
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> pending_dev: Review wrapper PASS but auto-merge failed (INV-33)
transitions.note: autonomous retained so dispatcher Step 4 re-dispatches dev to rebase.

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh mergeable-gate CONFLICTING: --remove-label reviewing --add-label pending-dev
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> pending_dev: Review wrapper PASS but PR CONFLICTING (mergeable gate, INV-44)
transitions.note: _classify_mergeable_gate → block-substantive. Hoisted PR-open guard runs first (INV-54).

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh mergeable-gate UNKNOWN: --remove-label reviewing --add-label pending-dev
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> pending_dev: Review wrapper PASS but mergeable UNKNOWN (mergeable gate, INV-44)
transitions.note: _classify_mergeable_gate → block-nonsubstantive (cause=mergeable-unknown). autonomous retained → dispatcher flips back to pending-review.

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh E2E gate FAIL same-HEAD breaker: --remove-label reviewing --add-label stalled
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> stalled: E2E hard gate FAIL repeated on unchanged (head, rc) >= GATE_FAIL_STALL_THRESHOLD [INV-122]
transitions.note: INV-122 (#453): the same-HEAD circuit breaker for a REPEATED INV-46 E2E-gate fail against an unchanged (head, rc) pair. Runs INSIDE the existing E2E_GATE==fail branch, BEFORE the review-e2e-gate-fail transition below — a trip short-circuits that transition entirely via label_swap reviewing→stalled FIRST (atomic, mirrors INV-105's TOCTOU fix), then posts exactly ONE reason=same-head-gate-failure report. Checks the issue is not already stalled (e.g. INV-105 tripped first) before running. Fingerprint is (head_sha, e2e_lane_rc), not head alone — a different rc on the same head resets the counter rather than accumulating it, so an unrelated transient failure followed by a genuinely new substantive bug on the same untouched head is never misclassified as non-convergent. Deliberately does NOT gate on may_stall_now/pid-dead (codex review round 2 [P1]): that predicate's dispatch-marker-freshness check exists for the DISPATCHER to ask whether an EXTERNAL wrapper might still be alive; this breaker instead runs synchronously inside the very review wrapper the dispatcher just launched, so it would always see its own fresh review-mode dispatch marker and defer for the full TTL, silently defeating the breaker for any E2E failure completing within that window. The reviewing-label single-writer invariant already provides the liveness guarantee may_stall_now exists to add.

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh E2E gate FAIL: --remove-label reviewing --add-label pending-dev
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> pending_dev: E2E hard gate FAIL — no review fan-out (INV-46, only if PR still OPEN per INV-54)
transitions.note: _classify_e2e_gate → fail. Runs BEFORE the review fan-out; agents NOT spawned. When the review-e2e-gate-fail-breaker above trips, THIS transition does not fire (short-circuited).

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh E2E gate BLOCK (evidence missing): --remove-label reviewing --add-label pending-dev
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> pending_dev: E2E evidence missing after lane — re-queue (INV-46, only if PR still OPEN per INV-54)
transitions.note: _classify_e2e_gate → block-nonsubstantive (cause=e2e-evidence-missing, transient).

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh cleanup() crash path: --remove-label reviewing --add-label pending-dev
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> pending_dev: Review wrapper trap (crash exit non-zero)
transitions.note: Fires only when RESULT_PARSED=false (distinct from the smoke-config-error self-heal which sets RESULT_PARSED=true).

transitions.code_site: dispatcher-tick.sh Step 5b: label_swap "$issue_num" "reviewing" "pending-dev"
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> pending_dev: Dispatcher Step 5b (DEAD reviewing)
transitions.note: Same DEAD contract as the dev side (INV-24).

transitions.code_site: dispatcher-tick.sh Step 4: label_swap "$issue_num" "pending-dev" "in-progress"
transitions.mermaid: pending_dev --> in_progress: Dispatcher Step 4 scan-pending-dev (retries below MAX)
transitions.note: Includes the prompt_too_long fresh-dev recovery branch (also a pending-dev→in-progress swap).

transitions.code_site: lib-dispatch.sh handle_completed_session_routing(): label_swap "$issue_num" "pending-dev" "in-progress"
transitions.mermaid: pending_dev --> in_progress: Step 4 review-aware (completed session + substantive review failure → dev-new) [INV-35]
transitions.note: Prior session end_turn|completed + substantive review-failure newer than session end → fresh dev-new. Per-issue log truncated (fail-closed).

transitions.code_site: lib-dispatch.sh handle_completed_session_routing(): label_swap "$issue_num" "pending-dev" "in-progress"
transitions.mermaid: pending_dev --> in_progress: Step 4 review-aware (completed session, verdict=none, no PR → dev-new) [INV-123]
transitions.note: #456/#461: prior session end_turn|completed + verdict=none (no qualifying review comment found) + NO PR exists for the issue → fresh dev-new, mirroring the substantive Branch C dispatch mechanics exactly (distinct marker INV-12-no-pr-fresh-dev:<sid>, no per-HEAD attempt marker — there is no HEAD). A none verdict WITH a PR existing takes the unchanged INV-12-completed operator-handoff path instead (no transition, not this row).

transitions.code_site: lib-dispatch.sh handle_completed_session_routing(): label_swap "$issue_num" "pending-dev" "pending-review"
transitions.mermaid: pending_dev --> pending_review: Step 4 review-aware (completed session + non-substantive review failure, under retry cap) [INV-35]
transitions.note: Non-substantive flip count < REVIEW_RETRY_LIMIT (default 2).

transitions.code_site: lib-dispatch.sh handle_pending_dev_pr_exists(): label_swap "$issue_num" "pending-dev" "pending-review"
transitions.mermaid: pending_dev --> pending_review: Step 4 PR-exists short-circuit (a PR already references this issue)
transitions.note: If a PR already references the issue, skip re-dispatching dev and route straight to review.

transitions.code_site: lib-dispatch.sh _same_head_verdict_aware_recovery() {
transitions.mermaid: pending_dev --> in_progress: Step 4a.5 self-heal (same-HEAD FAILED verdict + no resolvable session id + no live wrapper -> bounded dev-new) [INV-111]
transitions.note: Session-report comment was lost (e.g. auth-shim teardown race, #402) so extract_dev_session_id is empty and INV-98's delegation can never fire. Bounded to ONE dev-new per unchanged HEAD via a shared self-heal-lost-session:<head> / crashed-session-retry:<head> budget ([INV-125], #466); a spent budget or non-substantive re-review cap now escalates to mark_stalled instead of the residual park.

transitions.code_site: lib-dispatch.sh _same_head_verdict_aware_recovery() { reviewed FAILED (${cause}) with no live wrapper
transitions.mermaid: pending_dev --> in_progress: Step 4a.5 crashed-session recovery (same-HEAD FAILED verdict + resolved session id whose completion is unprovable + no live wrapper -> bounded dev-new) [INV-125]
transitions.note: #466: a resolved dev session id whose `is_session_completed` check returned false (non-terminal stop reason such as api_error, non-claude dev CLI, or unreadable log) with no live wrapper — the shared _same_head_verdict_aware_recovery helper's OTHER disjoint precondition from dispatch-pending-dev-selfheal-lost-session (session id resolved vs. empty). Shares the SAME per-HEAD dev-new budget (self-heal-lost-session:<head> OR crashed-session-retry:<head>, either blocks the other) so neither cause can double-spend the one bounded retry; a spent budget escalates to mark_stalled instead of the residual park.

transitions.code_site: lib-dispatch.sh mark_stalled(): --remove-label pending-dev --add-label stalled
transitions.mermaid: pending_dev --> stalled: Dispatcher Step 4 (retries at MAX)
transitions.note: retry count >= MAX_RETRIES after stalled-cutoff filtering.

transitions.code_site: lib-dispatch.sh mark_stalled() (from handle_completed_session_routing review cap branch)
transitions.mermaid: pending_dev --> stalled: Step 4 review-aware (non-substantive review-retry cap reached) [INV-35]
transitions.note: Non-substantive flip count >= REVIEW_RETRY_LIMIT.

transitions.code_site: lib-dispatch.sh mark_stalled() (from handle_completed_session_routing failed-substantive no-progress / bot-unfixable branch)
transitions.mermaid: pending_dev --> stalled: Step 4 review-aware (completed session + substantive review failure, no HEAD progress or bot-unfixable) [INV-85]
transitions.note: INV-85 (#274): completed-session failed-substantive escalates to stalled instead of looping dev-new when the prior dev-new made no commit against the unchanged HEAD, or the dev agent hit a PR-metadata 403 (bot-unfixable). Bounded to one dev-new per HEAD.

transitions.code_site: lib-dispatch.sh handle_completed_session_routing Branch B″ convergence circuit-breaker (label_swap pending-dev → stalled runs FIRST, atomically BEFORE the reason=non-convergence report + marker are posted — round-10 [P1] fix)
transitions.mermaid: pending_dev --> stalled: Step 4 convergence breaker (completed session + substantive review failure + frozen HEAD >=N completed zero-commit rounds) [INV-105]
transitions.note: INV-105 (#297): the deterministic >=N belt to INV-85's single-shot suspenders. A failed-substantive + dev-actionable=true verdict that churns >=CONVERGENCE_STALL_THRESHOLD (default 3) completed zero-commit dev-resume rounds against a frozen PR head is halted: this pending-dev -> stalled transition (via label_swap, NOT mark_stalled, so exactly one terminal comment) lands FIRST, then one reason=non-convergence report + marker is posted (transition-then-report ordering closes a TOCTOU where a failed transition could leave an orphan marker). Gated behind the shared may_stall_now live-PID pre-gate (pid-dead). autonomous is retained throughout — never removed by this movement.

transitions.code_site: lib-dispatch.sh _liveness_evaluate_issue() tier2 case (label_swap runs FIRST, atomically BEFORE the reason=liveness-timeout report + marker are posted)
transitions.mermaid: pending_dev --> stalled: Step 6 liveness watchdog (observable-state fingerprint frozen >=LIVENESS_STALL_TICKS ticks) [INV-128]
transitions.note: INV-128 (#467): the class-level backstop for the 'permanent silent park' bug class (INV-105/111/122/123/125 were per-entry point-fixes). Any pending-dev issue whose observable state fingerprint (label, PR head, non-idempotent comment count, marker digest) is unchanged for >=LIVENESS_STALL_TICKS (default 18) consecutive ticks is halted: this pending-dev -> stalled transition (via label_swap) lands FIRST, then one reason=liveness-timeout report + marker is posted (mirrors INV-105/INV-122's TOCTOU-safe transition-then-report ordering). Re-checks current labels for stalled immediately before acting (mirrors INV-122's already-stalled skip) — a specific breaker may have won the race. autonomous is retained throughout — never removed by this movement.

transitions.code_site: lib-dispatch.sh _liveness_evaluate_issue() tier2 case (label_swap runs FIRST, atomically BEFORE the reason=liveness-timeout report + marker are posted)
transitions.mermaid: pending_review --> stalled: Step 6 liveness watchdog (observable-state fingerprint frozen >=LIVENESS_STALL_TICKS ticks) [INV-128]
transitions.note: INV-128 (#467): the pending-review sibling of liveness-watchdog-stall-pending-dev — a genuinely NEW movement (no prior transition declared pending_review -> stalled). A pending-review issue whose observable state fingerprint is unchanged for >=LIVENESS_STALL_TICKS (default 18) consecutive ticks is halted the same way. autonomous is retained throughout.

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh PASS no-auto-close: --remove-label reviewing --add-label approved
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> approved: Review PASS + no-auto-close (PR open, awaiting manual merge)
transitions.note: Human-edit event: maintainer added no-auto-close. autonomous retained; PR awaits manual merge.

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh PASS approval-failed: --remove-label reviewing --add-label approved
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> approved: Review PASS but gh pr review --approve failed (manual approval needed)
transitions.note: Verdict matched but the approve API returned non-zero; labels still flip.

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh no-PR early exit: --remove-label reviewing --add-label pending-dev
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> pending_dev: Review found no PR (all 3 discovery methods failed)
transitions.note: Early exit before fan-out when no PR references the issue.

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh _pr_open_gate → skip: --remove-label reviewing (no add)
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> reviewing: Concurrent merge — PR no longer OPEN, silent −reviewing (INV-54)
transitions.note: Hoisted PR-open guard at the PASS-chain top AND the E2E hard gate. Remove-only (no add) — another review/merge wrote approved or merged first. Self-loop in the diagram = no net active-state add.

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh Phase A.5 _classify_smoke_gate → fail: sets RESULT_PARSED=true, exits non-zero, NO label edit
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> reviewing: Pre-fan-out smoke FAIL (config error) — stays reviewing, self-heals (INV-64)
transitions.note: Operator-side config error, not a PR defect. RESULT_PARSED=true so the crash trap does NOT flip +pending-dev. Re-runs next tick once config fixed.

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh mandatory-bot-review gate: missing_bot_reviews non-empty AND wait count < BOT_REVIEW_WAIT_MAX → --remove-label reviewing --add-label pending-review (non-substantive re-queue)
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> pending_review: PASS but a mandatory REVIEW_BOTS review is still missing — re-queue for re-review (INV-79, scoped mode; bounded by BOT_REVIEW_WAIT_MAX)
transitions.note: INV-79 scoped mode: the agent brokers its bot trigger in cleanup, so a PASS could be approved before the bot reviews. Re-queue pending-review (NOT pending-dev — the code is fine, only awaiting the async bot review; pending-dev's #106 stale-verdict guard would STALL). Bounded by the SHA-bound bot-review-wait marker count vs BOT_REVIEW_WAIT_MAX (default 3).

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh mandatory-bot-review gate: missing_bot_reviews non-empty AND wait count ≥ BOT_REVIEW_WAIT_MAX → request-changes + --remove-label reviewing --add-label pending-dev (substantive FAIL)
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> pending_dev: Mandatory bot review absent after BOT_REVIEW_WAIT_MAX waits — substantive FAIL (INV-79)
transitions.note: INV-79 scoped mode: the configured REVIEW_BOTS never reviewed after BOT_REVIEW_WAIT_MAX brokered trigger attempts on this HEAD — the bot is misconfigured/rate-limited/down. Substantive FAIL: gh pr review --request-changes + pending-dev. A maintainer re-triggers once the bot is healthy, or drops it from REVIEW_BOTS.

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh CI-rollup gate: _cir_head_matches mismatch (checked before AND after chp_ci_rollup) → --remove-label reviewing --add-label pending-review (non-substantive re-queue, never approves)
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> pending_review: CI-rollup gate — reviewed HEAD changed mid-round, never approve a stale HEAD (INV-134)
transitions.note: The PR's HEAD advanced (a new dev push) between when this review round started and when the CI-rollup gate ran — chp_ci_rollup and chp_approve are both skipped. autonomous retained → dispatcher re-reviews against the new head. Posts the INV-129 round=0 reset marker.

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh CI-rollup gate: _classify_ci_rollup_gate → block-substantive (token=failed) → request-changes + --remove-label reviewing --add-label pending-dev
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> pending_dev: Review wrapper PASS but a red check on the reviewed HEAD (CI-rollup gate, INV-134)
transitions.note: _classify_ci_rollup_gate → block-substantive. Finding names every entry in failed_checks. A re-push re-triggers CI. chp_approve is never reached.

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh CI-rollup gate: token=pending or leaf rc≠0, SHA-bound wait count < CI_ROLLUP_WAIT_MAX → --remove-label reviewing --add-label pending-review (non-substantive re-queue)
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> pending_review: PASS but CI checks still pending / status unavailable — re-queue for re-review (INV-134, bounded by CI_ROLLUP_WAIT_MAX)
transitions.note: No new commits exist yet, so pending-dev's stale-verdict guard would stall the hold — pending-review re-queues a clean re-review on the next tick. Bounded by the SHA-bound ci-rollup-wait marker count vs CI_ROLLUP_WAIT_MAX (default 3), mirrors INV-79's bot-review-wait mechanics.

transitions.code_site: autonomous-review.sh CI-rollup gate: wait count ≥ CI_ROLLUP_WAIT_MAX → request-changes + --remove-label reviewing --add-label pending-dev (substantive FAIL)
transitions.mermaid: reviewing --> pending_dev: CI checks still not clear after CI_ROLLUP_WAIT_MAX waits — substantive FAIL (INV-134)
transitions.note: The check never resolved (stuck/misconfigured) after CI_ROLLUP_WAIT_MAX brokered waits on this HEAD. Substantive FAIL: gh pr review --request-changes + pending-dev, mirrors the INV-79 bot-gate give-up.

transitions.code_site: lib-dispatch.sh hygiene_strip_residual_labels(): dynamic --remove-label of any transitional label co-residing with a terminal label
transitions.mermaid: approved --> approved: Step 0 hygiene strips transitional residue (INV-25)
transitions.note: Sticky terminal state. Strips any of {in-progress, reviewing, pending-review, pending-dev} co-residing with approved.

transitions.code_site: lib-dispatch.sh hygiene_strip_residual_labels(): dynamic --remove-label of any transitional label co-residing with a terminal label
transitions.mermaid: stalled --> stalled: Step 0 hygiene strips transitional residue (INV-25)
transitions.note: Same hygiene rule as approved.

transitions.mermaid: stalled --> autonomous: Maintainer removes stalled label (autonomous retained → re-enters via Step 2; retry counter resets, INV-05)
transitions.note: Human-edit event: removing ONLY the stalled label. mark_stalled() never removed autonomous (only review-pass-merged removes it), so the issue is left autonomous-only and re-enters through dispatcher Step 2 list_new_issues → dispatch-new (autonomous → in_progress) on the next tick. The retry counter resets (INV-05). Modeling this as stalled → pending_dev would be wrong: no pending-dev label is ever written on this path.

transitions.mermaid: approved --> [*]: PR merged (auto or manual)
transitions.note: Terminal. GitHub closes the issue via Closes #N when the PR merges.

diagram.title: Issue Label State Machine — GENERATED from transitions.json

lines: Step 0 hygiene at the next tick.

invalid_combinations.description: Generated as the complement of the legal active-state set: an issue carries at most ONE active-state label. Any pair of active labels (and any terminal+active pair) is forbidden. See state-machine.md § Forbidden transitions for the prose contract and the four headline cases.
invalid_combinations.rule: An issue holds at most one of active_states; a terminal_state co-resides with no active_state (self-healed at Step 0 hygiene, INV-25).
